name: 穆齐奥·克莱门蒂
brief: 克莱门蒂出生于罗马，14岁时被英国绅士彼得·贝克福德「买下」带到英国培养，从此定居伦敦。他不仅是杰出的钢琴家和作曲家，还是成功的出版商、钢琴制造商和教育家。1781年他在维也纳与莫扎特进行了著名的钢琴竞赛——约瑟夫二世邀请两人当面比试。克莱门蒂晚年功成名就，去世时被葬于威斯敏斯特教堂，与亨德尔和珀塞尔比邻。
briefEn: Clementi was born in Rome and at fourteen was effectively 'purchased' by the English gentleman Peter Beckford, who brought him to England for his musical education. He settled in London permanently, becoming not only a distinguished pianist and composer but also a successful music publisher, piano manufacturer, and educator. In 1781 he took part in a famous keyboard contest with Mozart in Vienna, arranged by Emperor Joseph II. Clementi enjoyed great success in his later years and upon his death was buried in Westminster Abbey, alongside Handel and Purcell.
contribution: 克莱门蒂被称为「钢琴音乐之父」。他的《名手之道》（Gradus ad Parnassum）是钢琴技巧训练的百科全书，至今仍是钢琴教学的基石。他是第一位真正为钢琴（而非大键琴）而写的作曲家，充分发掘了钢琴的力度变化、踏板效果和歌唱性能力。他的钢琴奏鸣曲在某些方面比莫扎特更加大胆前卫。
contributionEn: Clementi is known as the 'Father of Piano Music.' His Gradus ad Parnassum is an encyclopedic compendium of piano technique that remains a cornerstone of piano pedagogy to this day. He was the first composer to write truly for the piano rather than the harpsichord, fully exploiting the instrument's dynamic range, pedal capabilities, and singing tone. In certain respects his piano sonatas were bolder and more forward-looking than Mozart's.
style: 克莱门蒂的早期作品以华丽的技巧性见长——快速的八度、三度和六度平行进行、大幅度跳跃等技法都是他的标志。成熟期的作品则展现出更深邃的音乐思考，和声语汇丰富，结构创新大胆。他的慢乐章具有真挚的歌唱性，快乐章则兼具辉煌的技巧与扎实的音乐内容。
styleEn: Clementi's early works are marked by brilliant virtuosity — rapid parallel octaves, thirds, and sixths, along with wide leaps that became his trademarks. His mature compositions reveal deeper musical thought, with a rich harmonic vocabulary and boldly innovative structures. His slow movements possess a genuine singing quality, while his fast movements combine dazzling technique with substantial musical content.

famousScores.name: 《名手之道》 Gradus ad Parnassum
famousScores.desc: 100首钢琴练习曲，钢琴技巧训练的终极宝典
famousScores.descEn: 100 piano etudes — the definitive compendium of keyboard technique
famousScores.detail:
《名手之道》（Gradus ad Parnassum，意为「通往帕尔纳索斯山之阶梯」）分三册出版，第一册（1817年）、第二册（1819年）、第三册（1826年），共收录100首练习曲，涵盖了钢琴技巧的几乎所有方面。克莱门蒂从70岁写到74岁才完成这套皇皇巨作，书名直接借用了1725年对位学大师约翰·约瑟夫·福克斯的同名论著，显示了他对这部作品历史地位的自信。

在钢琴教学史上，这套练习曲几乎立刻成为了标准教材。克莱门蒂的学生卡尔·车尔尼以它为模本，创作了自己的系列练习曲；车尔尼的学生李斯特则进一步将克莱门蒂的技巧传统发展为19世纪的钢琴演奏体系。德彪西在他1910年创作的12首钢琴前奏曲中，戏谑地引用了《名手之道》中最典型的「练习曲织体」，在序曲《博士的步态》（Doctor Gradus ad Parnassum）中以波浪形的手指练习旋律嘲讽枯燥的钢琴练习，这首曲子成为了克莱门蒂这部作品在流行文化中最为人知的一个注脚。

尽管《名手之道》作为练习曲集被广泛使用，但其中真正优秀的作品具有相当的艺术价值，不仅仅是技术练习。其中的缓慢练习曲如第2号（D大调）和第9号（C大调行板）具有深刻的歌唱性，是克莱门蒂慢乐章写作水平的集中体现。现代钢琴家如弗拉基米尔·霍罗维茨的老师米莱伯格曾将《名手之道》推崇为可与巴赫《平均律》媲美的键盘文献。
famousScores.detailEn:
Gradus ad Parnassum ('Steps to Mount Parnassus') was published in three volumes (1817, 1819, 1826), containing 100 studies covering virtually every aspect of piano technique. Clementi labored on it from age seventy to seventy-four, borrowing his title from the celebrated 1725 counterpoint treatise by Johann Joseph Fux — a quiet declaration of confidence in the work's lasting significance.

The collection became a standard pedagogical text almost immediately. Clementi's pupil Carl Czerny used it as a model for his own study series; Czerny's pupil Liszt carried the Clementian technical tradition forward into the 19th-century virtuoso pianistic system. Debussy paid ironic homage in 1910 with 'Doctor Gradus ad Parnassum,' the wryly self-mocking opening piece of his Children's Corner suite, in which the undulating finger-exercise texture of the Gradus is gently satirized — the most widely known reference to Clementi in popular culture.

Though used primarily as technical study material, the finest pieces in the Gradus have genuine artistic substance. The slow studies, such as No. 2 in D major and No. 9 in C major (Andante), display Clementi's gift for singing melody at its most refined. Pianists including Horowitz's teacher Milberger have placed the Gradus alongside Bach's Well-Tempered Clavier as indispensable keyboard literature.

famousScores.name: 钢琴奏鸣曲 Op.36
famousScores.desc: 六首小奏鸣曲，几乎每个学琴的孩子都弹过
famousScores.descEn: Six sonatinas that nearly every piano student has played
famousScores.detail:
《钢琴小奏鸣曲》Op.36共六首，出版于1797年，是克莱门蒂专为初学者量身定制的教学作品。尽管被称为「小奏鸣曲」（Sonatina），但每首都是完整的、精心制作的微型奏鸣曲，通常包括两到三个乐章，难度从第一首（C大调，极易上手）到第六首（D大调，已有相当技术要求）逐渐递进。这套作品在克莱门蒂的众多作品中最为大众所知，是全球最广泛使用的入门教材之一。

克莱门蒂在这套小奏鸣曲中展示了他作为教育家的卓越天赋：他将古典奏鸣曲式的基本原则以最简练的方式呈现，同时保留了足够的音乐趣味使学生不感到枯燥。第一首C大调小奏鸣曲开篇的「格言式」动机（C-E-G-C的分解和弦）几乎已成为西方音乐启蒙教育的代名词；第三首G大调的行板以其优雅的歌唱旋律，是这套作品中情感最丰富的慢乐章。

19世纪至20世纪，这套小奏鸣曲被全球数以亿计的钢琴学生弹奏，成为继巴赫《初学者键盘小品》之后最广为人知的入门键盘教材。正是这套作品保证了克莱门蒂的名字在每一代人的钢琴启蒙记忆中都占有一席之地。讽刺的是，克莱门蒂自己将这套作品看得远不如他的大型奏鸣曲重要，它们作为小品在他在世时便大量出版，而他更重视的奏鸣曲却相对默默无闻。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Piano Sonatinas Op. 36 — six pieces published in 1797 — are Clementi's most universally known works, specifically designed for piano students at the beginning stages. Despite the diminutive title, each sonatina is a complete and carefully crafted miniature sonata, typically in two or three movements, progressing in difficulty from the very accessible first (C major) to the more demanding sixth (D major). They are among the most widely used introductory teaching materials in the world.

Clementi's genius as an educator shines through these sonatinas: the fundamental principles of Classical sonata form are presented with maximum clarity while retaining enough musical character to hold a young student's interest. The opening 'motto' of the First Sonatina (C-E-G-C arpeggio) has become almost synonymous with Western beginner piano education; the Andante of the Third (G major) is the most emotionally expressive slow movement of the set.

Played by hundreds of millions of piano students worldwide over two centuries, the Op. 36 Sonatinas are, after Bach's Little Notebook for Wilhelm Friedemann, the most universally known introductory keyboard pieces in the Western canon. Ironically, Clementi himself regarded them as trifles compared to his major sonatas; yet it is these modest pieces that have kept his name alive in the memory of every generation of piano students.

famousScores.name: 钢琴奏鸣曲 Op.13 No.6 F小调
famousScores.nameEn: Piano Sonata in F minor, Op. 13 No. 6
famousScores.desc: 被贝多芬称赞的深刻之作，预示了浪漫主义
famousScores.descEn: A work of profound depth praised by Beethoven, foreshadowing Romanticism
famousScores.detail:
《f小调钢琴奏鸣曲》Op.13 No.6出版于约1784年，是克莱门蒂早期成熟期的重要作品。这首奏鸣曲以f小调写成，已显示出克莱门蒂超越纯技巧炫耀的更深层音乐追求。第一乐章（急板）以坚定的主部主题开始，那种阴郁的驱动力在莫扎特时代的钢琴音乐中是十分罕见的，令人想到日后贝多芬的「热情」奏鸣曲风格。第二乐章（慢板或如歌的行板）则展现了克莱门蒂慢乐章写作的深情与诗意。

贝多芬据说专门向弟子们推荐克莱门蒂的奏鸣曲，认为其中有真正深刻的音乐思想，胜过大部分同时代的钢琴作品，对于Op.13的系列作品尤为推崇。车尔尼在其回忆录中也记述，贝多芬在早期对克莱门蒂奏鸣曲的研习是他自身风格形成的重要来源之一，而Op.13 No.6 f小调奏鸣曲以其小调情绪和坚定的意志力，与贝多芬的审美趣味格外契合。

这首奏鸣曲是理解克莱门蒂在钢琴文献发展史上重要意义的关键作品之一。作为一首写于1784年的f小调作品，它已经具备了贝多芬「热情」奏鸣曲（Op.57，1804年）精神气质的某些萌芽——小调的宿命感、演奏上的力量要求、情感的内在紧张性。从这个意义上说，克莱门蒂不是受贝多芬影响的人，而恰恰相反，是贝多芬从他身上汲取了最初的营养。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Piano Sonata in F minor Op. 13 No. 6 was published around 1784 and marks an important stage in Clementi's early maturity. Written in a brooding F minor, the first movement drives forward with a muscular intensity rarely found in piano music of the Mozart era — anticipating the spirit of Beethoven's 'Appassionata' sonata. The slow movement (Lento or Andante cantabile) reveals Clementi's gift for lyrical, emotionally resonant melody at its most eloquent.

Beethoven reportedly recommended Clementi's sonatas to his pupils specifically because they contained genuine musical depth, superior to most contemporary keyboard music; the Op. 13 set was among those he most admired. Czerny recorded in his memoirs that Beethoven's early study of Clementi sonatas was a significant influence on his own developing style — and the F minor sonata, with its minor-key fatalism and driven energy, accorded particularly closely with Beethoven's aesthetic instincts.

This sonata is one of the key works for understanding Clementi's historically important role in the development of piano music. Written in F minor in 1784, it already contains certain seeds of the spiritual character of Beethoven's 'Appassionata' (Op. 57, 1804) — the sense of minor-key destiny, the physical demands of performance, the interior emotional tension. In this respect, the influence ran from Clementi to Beethoven, not the reverse.

famousScores.name: 钢琴奏鸣曲 Op.40 No.2 B小调
famousScores.nameEn: Piano Sonata in B minor, Op. 40 No. 2
famousScores.desc: 晚期杰作，结构宏大、情感深沉
famousScores.descEn: A late masterpiece of grand architecture and deep emotional gravity
famousScores.detail:
《b小调钢琴奏鸣曲》Op.40 No.2是克莱门蒂晚期最重要的奏鸣曲之一，约出版于1802年。这首奏鸣曲以b小调写成，在情感深度和结构规模上都代表了克莱门蒂成熟期创作的最高水准。第一乐章宏大的主题以b小调的阴郁色调展开，发展部的转调极为大胆，使用了当时十分罕见的调性远关系，展现了克莱门蒂和声语汇的丰富与超前。

这首奏鸣曲创作于1800年前后，恰好处于古典主义向浪漫主义过渡的关键历史节点。与贝多芬同时代的克莱门蒂，在这一时期的奏鸣曲中已明显突破了古典和声的常规边界：连续的半音化和声进行、突然的调性跳跃、充满张力的长句结构，这些手法与贝多芬早中期奏鸣曲的风格遥相呼应，甚至有时更为大胆。

现代演奏家和学者对Op.40 No.2的重新发现，是20世纪克莱门蒂研究的重要成果之一。意大利钢琴家皮尔卡洛·斯卡米诺和蒂博·纳吉的录音让这首奏鸣曲重新进入了音乐会曲目。学者们在研究克莱门蒂的历史意义时，普遍将Op.40系列（尤其是No.2）列为理解他对贝多芬影响的核心证据，认为这一系列奏鸣曲已经超越了古典主义框架，是名副其实的「前浪漫主义」杰作。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Piano Sonata in B minor Op. 40 No. 2 is one of Clementi's most important late works, published around 1802. Written in B minor, it represents the summit of his mature compositional ambition in both structural scale and emotional depth. The first movement's grand opening theme unfolds with somber B-minor coloring; the development section employs startlingly remote tonal regions that were rare for their time, demonstrating the richness and advanced character of Clementi's harmonic language.

Composed around 1800, at the critical historical juncture between Classicism and Romanticism, this sonata clearly pushes beyond the norms of Classical harmony: sustained chromatic progressions, sudden tonal leaps, long-breathed lines of great tension — all of which parallel and sometimes outpace the style of Beethoven's early and middle-period sonatas.

The modern rediscovery of Op. 40 No. 2 is one of the significant achievements of 20th-century Clementi scholarship. Recordings by Italian pianists Piercarlo Scarmiglione and Tibor Szász restored it to the concert repertoire. Scholars studying Clementi's historical significance consistently cite the Op. 40 set (especially No. 2) as central evidence of his influence on Beethoven, arguing that these sonatas have genuinely transcended the Classical framework to merit the designation of 'pre-Romantic' masterworks.

famousScores.name: 《降B大调钢琴奏鸣曲》 Op.47 No.2
famousScores.nameEn: Piano Sonata in B-flat major, Op. 47 No. 2
famousScores.desc: 中期奏鸣曲，充满实验精神
famousScores.descEn: A middle-period sonata imbued with an adventurous experimental spirit
famousScores.detail:
《降B大调钢琴奏鸣曲》Op.47 No.2约出版于1820年，是克莱门蒂中晚期重要的钢琴独奏作品之一。降B大调是克莱门蒂颇为偏爱的调性，在这首奏鸣曲中他展现了成熟期标志性的写作手法：广阔的音域利用、丰富的踏板效果运用以及歌唱性的旋律线条与辉煌技巧段落的交织。第一乐章开篇的引子充满庄严的仪式感，随后的奏鸣曲快板以强烈的主题对比构建张力。

这首奏鸣曲写作时，克莱门蒂已年届七旬，但其中展现的创作活力和技术要求却丝毫不逊于他的早期作品。值得注意的是，这一时期克莱门蒂正同时经营他的钢琴出版和制造事业，其中包括为贝多芬担任英国出版商——他曾亲赴维也纳与贝多芬商谈出版合同，两人有过多次直接交流，这使得两人在艺术上的相互影响更加有据可查。

Op.47系列奏鸣曲在克莱门蒂重新被发现的过程中，是最晚得到重视的一批。今天，这首降B大调奏鸣曲被视为克莱门蒂探索踏板运用（他在某些奏鸣曲中留下了明确的踏板标记，这在当时极为罕见）和音域极限的重要文献，对于理解古典钢琴奏鸣曲向浪漫主义钢琴写作演变的过程具有重要的参考价值。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Piano Sonata in B-flat major Op. 47 No. 2 was published around 1820, one of Clementi's significant late solo piano works. B-flat major was a key Clementi favored, and this sonata shows his mature compositional fingerprints: a wide keyboard range, sophisticated pedal effects, interweaving of singing melodic lines with passages of brilliant virtuosity. The solemn introductory gesture of the first movement gives way to a sonata Allegro of strong thematic contrasts.

When Clementi composed this sonata he was in his seventies, yet the creative energy and technical demands are undiminished. Notably, at this period Clementi was simultaneously active as Beethoven's English publisher, having traveled to Vienna personally to negotiate contracts — the two composers had multiple direct exchanges, making their mutual artistic influence unusually well documented.

The Op. 47 sonatas were among the last of Clementi's works to receive serious critical attention in the modern revival of his music. Today, this B-flat major sonata is valued as an important document of Clementi's exploration of pedal technique (he left explicit pedaling marks in certain sonatas — highly unusual for his time) and keyboard range, offering a revealing perspective on how Classical piano writing evolved toward the Romantic style.

famousScores.name: 《f升小调钢琴奏鸣曲》 Op.25 No.5「被遗弃的狄多」
famousScores.nameEn: Piano Sonata in F-sharp minor, Op. 25 No. 5 'Didone abbandonata'
famousScores.desc: 以维吉尔史诗女主角命名，是克莱门蒂最富戏剧性的奏鸣曲
famousScores.descEn: Named after the tragic heroine of Virgil's Aeneid, Clementi's most dramatically intense sonata
famousScores.detail:
《f升小调钢琴奏鸣曲》Op.25 No.5约出版于1790年，副标题「被遗弃的狄多」（Didone abbandonata）取自维吉尔《埃涅阿斯纪》中迦太基女王狄多被埃涅阿斯遗弃后自焚的悲剧故事。这是克莱门蒂极少数带有标题的奏鸣曲之一，也是他的奏鸣曲中最具浪漫主义戏剧气质的一首。f升小调本身已是极具个性的调性——肖邦后来在这个调性写下了他最著名的练习曲——克莱门蒂则比肖邦早了近半个世纪在此调性中探索黑暗与激情。

这首奏鸣曲的第一乐章以一个充满悲剧预感的引子开始，随后急板冲出，以快速的平行八度和猛烈的力度对比描绘狄多内心的绝望。中间的行板乐章是这首奏鸣曲的情感核心——曲调哀婉，和声游移，仿佛是女王在最后夜晚的独白。终乐章的激情急板彻底爆发，以近乎粗暴的打击感结束，象征着命运的无情和自我毁灭。

这首奏鸣曲在克莱门蒂作品中一向被低估，直到20世纪后半叶才开始引起学者和演奏家的关注。值得一提的是，克莱门蒂后来在Op.50中又创作了另一首同名「Didone abbandonata」奏鸣曲（g小调，更为著名），但Op.25 No.5因其更为直接的情感冲击力而具有独特的地位，是研究克莱门蒂奏鸣曲风格早期激情面貌的最佳窗口。
famousScores.detailEn:
Piano Sonata in F-sharp minor Op. 25 No. 5, subtitled 'Didone abbandonata' ('Dido Abandoned'), was published around 1790. The subtitle refers to the tragic Carthaginian queen of Virgil's Aeneid, who immolates herself after being deserted by Aeneas — one of the few programmatic titles Clementi ever used, and the one that most clearly declares the Romantic dramatic ambitions of his mature style. F-sharp minor was an exceptionally distinctive key choice: Chopin would later use it for his most celebrated etude, but Clementi explored its dark passion nearly half a century earlier.

The first movement opens with a grave introduction full of tragic foreboding before the Allegro erupts, painting Dido's desperation through rapid parallel octaves and violent dynamic contrasts. The central Adagio is the emotional heart of the sonata — a wandering, harmonically unstable lament suggesting the queen's last soliloquy in the night. The finale breaks into passionate agitation, concluding with a force that suggests inexorable fate and self-destruction.

This sonata has long been undervalued among Clementi's works, attracting serious scholarly attention only from the latter half of the 20th century. Clementi later composed another 'Didone abbandonata' sonata in G minor (Op. 50 No. 3), which became more famous; but the F-sharp minor Op. 25 No. 5 holds its own unique place as the earliest and most direct expression of Clementi's capacity for emotional drama, offering the clearest window into the passionate early face of his sonata style.

famousScores.name: 《D大调第二交响曲》 WoO 34
famousScores.nameEn: Symphony No. 2 in D major, WoO 34
famousScores.desc: 克莱门蒂最重要的交响曲，展示了他不为人知的管弦乐才能
famousScores.descEn: Clementi's most important symphony, revealing an orchestral mastery rarely appreciated
famousScores.detail:
克莱门蒂共创作了四首交响曲，其中《D大调第二交响曲》（WoO 34，约1820年代）是保存最完整、艺术价值最高的一首。这部交响曲的存在本身就令人惊讶——克莱门蒂以钢琴作曲家和教育家著称，他的管弦乐创作在历史上几乎被完全忽视，但这首交响曲证明他同样具备驾驭大型管弦乐队的能力。作品以D大调的明朗开始，第一乐章的奏鸣曲式结构严谨而流畅，主题发展手法颇具海顿-莫扎特传统的精巧。

这首交响曲创作于贝多芬交响曲的全盛时代，克莱门蒂对此自然有所意识。他的管弦乐写法与贝多芬的英雄主义风格不同，更倾向于海顿式的清澈与优雅。第二乐章（行板或慢板）展现了类似于他钢琴慢乐章的歌唱性，弦乐旋律线条饱满而富于色彩。终乐章的回旋曲充满活力，大量运用对位手法，显示了克莱门蒂从《名手之道》的教学实践中提炼出来的对位技艺。

克莱门蒂的交响曲是近年来音乐学界重新评估他历史地位的重要证据之一。20世纪末，意大利指挥家皮耶罗·戈里的录音首次向现代听众展示了这些作品的面貌，引发了学界对克莱门蒂作为多才多艺的古典主义作曲家的重新讨论。今天，他的交响曲研究已成为18世纪晚期英国古典音乐研究的重要议题，填补了那个时代英国交响曲史上的一段空白。
famousScores.detailEn:
Clementi composed four symphonies, of which the Symphony No. 2 in D major (WoO 34, c. 1820s) is the best preserved and most musically significant. Its very existence surprises many listeners accustomed to thinking of Clementi solely as a keyboard figure: this work demonstrates a genuine command of the full orchestra. The first movement is tightly structured in sonata form, with thematic development that carries on the Haydn-Mozart tradition with grace and ingenuity.

Composed at the height of Beethoven's symphonic dominance, Clementi's approach is deliberately different — closer to the transparent elegance of Haydn than to Beethoven's heroic grand manner. The slow movement shows the same quality of singing string melody found in his finest piano slow movements. The rondo finale is spirited and contrapuntally rich, drawing on the polyphonic craft he had refined through decades of pedagogical work in the Gradus.

Clementi's symphonies have become important evidence in the recent scholarly reassessment of his historical stature. Late-20th-century recordings by Italian conductor Piero Galli introduced these works to modern audiences for the first time, sparking renewed scholarly discussion of Clementi as a multi-faceted Classical composer. His symphonies now constitute a significant topic in the study of late 18th-century British orchestral music, filling a largely forgotten chapter in the history of the English Classical symphony.

famousScores.name: 《C大调钢琴奏鸣曲》 Op.34 No.1
famousScores.nameEn: Piano Sonata in C major, Op. 34 No. 1
famousScores.desc: 克莱门蒂成熟期的辉煌之作，以C大调的恢宏气度展现大师技艺
famousScores.descEn: A magnificent mature work radiating the grandeur and mastery of Clementi at his zenith
famousScores.detail:
《C大调钢琴奏鸣曲》Op.34 No.1出版于约1795年，是克莱门蒂成熟期最具代表性的奏鸣曲之一。C大调是钢琴音乐的「王者调性」，克莱门蒂在这首奏鸣曲中充分发挥了这一调性的宏伟潜力。第一乐章以宽广而庄重的主部主题开场，低音区的分解和弦支撑起上方旋律的宏大气势，令人联想到管风琴音乐的庄严。发展部充分运用了转调和对位技术，将主题材料推向各个调性领域，展示了克莱门蒂成熟的和声驾驭能力。

这首奏鸣曲创作时期，克莱门蒂正处于他演奏生涯的巅峰，也是他欧洲声誉最隆的时期。1794-1795年间他再度游历欧洲，在柏林、圣彼得堡等地举行演出，颇受贵族与皇室的追捧。这首奏鸣曲很可能在这次巡演期间有过演出，作品中那种辉煌的公众性格也与这一时期的演出场合相吻合——它不是沙龙小品，而是为音乐厅而生的大型展示曲。

Op.34这套奏鸣曲被19世纪的钢琴教学体系列为高级教材，同时也是演奏家曲目中的珍品。在20世纪的克莱门蒂复兴中，这首C大调奏鸣曲是最早被重新发掘并录制的作品之一。钢琴家亚历山大·坦斯曼等人的录音帮助其重返音乐会舞台，证明了克莱门蒂的成熟奏鸣曲具有不输于同时代任何作品的演奏价值和听众吸引力。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Piano Sonata in C major Op. 34 No. 1 was published around 1795 and stands as one of the most representative works of Clementi's compositional maturity. C major is the 'royal key' of piano music, and Clementi exploits its grandeur to the full: the first movement opens with a broad, imposing main theme whose bass-register arpeggios support an upper melodic line of organ-like solemnity. The development section navigates distant harmonic regions with assured command, deploying modulation and counterpoint with the fluency of a fully matured master.

Clementi was at the height of his performing career and European reputation when he composed this sonata. During 1794–1795 he again toured the Continent, performing in Berlin and St. Petersburg to aristocratic and royal acclaim. This sonata almost certainly featured in these concerts — its brilliant, public character accords perfectly with the large venues and distinguished audiences of a major touring virtuoso. It is not salon music; it is music made for the concert hall.

The Op. 34 sonatas were adopted as advanced teaching material in 19th-century piano pedagogy, while also remaining fine concert pieces in their own right. In the 20th-century Clementi revival, this C major sonata was among the earliest to be rediscovered and recorded. Recordings by pianists including Alexander Tansman helped restore it to the concert stage, demonstrating that Clementi's mature sonatas reward both performers and audiences as fully as any keyboard work of the Classical era.

famousScores.name: 《A大调随想曲》 Op.17
famousScores.desc: 充满幽默与奇想的键盘小品，是克莱门蒂性格的另一面
famousScores.descEn: A witty and whimsical keyboard piece revealing Clementi's lighter, playful side
famousScores.detail:
《A大调随想曲》Op.17出版于约1785年，是克莱门蒂众多键盘小品中最具吸引力的一首。「随想曲」（Capriccio）是一种允许作曲家自由表达奇思妙想的体裁，在这首作品中，克莱门蒂展现了他鲜为人知的幽默与轻盈一面——与他雄厚深沉的大型奏鸣曲形成鲜明对比。A大调的明亮色彩搭配机智的旋律转折和出人意料的和声变化，使这首小品散发出一种乡村舞曲式的活泼气息。

这首随想曲在克莱门蒂的同时代作品中并不罕见——18世纪末的钢琴家们经常为贵族沙龙演出创作这类轻松悦耳的小品，以平衡大型奏鸣曲的严肃性。在风格上，它与海顿的一些键盘小品有异曲同工之妙：两位作曲家都善于在短小的篇幅内将幽默与音乐技巧完美融合。这首随想曲的中间段落出现了一段模仿民间管风琴的低音持续音效果，令人想起海顿晚期奏鸣曲中的类似手法。

《A大调随想曲》是克莱门蒂键盘作品多元面貌的绝佳代表，证明了他并不只是一位技巧至上的「练习曲作曲家」，也不只是追求宏大叙事的奏鸣曲作者，还是一位能够以轻盈笔触捕捉生活趣味的音乐家。在古典主义时代，「随想曲」体裁作为作曲家个人性情最自由的表达空间，往往比正式的奏鸣曲更直接地透露出作者的内心世界。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Capriccio in A major Op. 17, published around 1785, is one of the most appealing of Clementi's shorter keyboard pieces. The genre of the capriccio traditionally permitted composers to indulge their whims and wit, and Clementi does so here with a lightness rarely on display in his major sonatas: the brightness of A major, playful melodic turns, and unexpected harmonic shifts create a spirit close to rustic dance music, revealing a genial, humorous side of his personality.

Such lighter pieces were commonplace for late-18th-century pianists, who regularly supplied aristocratic salons with music to balance the weightier sonatas on their programs. In character, this Capriccio has much in common with certain Haydn keyboard miniatures: both composers excel at blending wit and technical polish within a compact form. A middle section imitating a drone bass — like a folk bagpipe or village organ — has a quality reminiscent of similar effects in Haydn's late sonatas.

The A major Capriccio is an ideal ambassador for the diversity of Clementi's keyboard output, proving that he was neither only a 'technician's composer' nor only a writer of grand narratives, but also a musician capable of capturing life's pleasures with a light touch. In the Classical era, the capriccio genre offered composers the freest imaginable space for personal expression — and in this one, Clementi's natural charm shines through with unmistakable warmth.

famousScores.name: 《d小调钢琴奏鸣曲》 Op.13 No.4
famousScores.nameEn: Piano Sonata in D minor, Op. 13 No. 4
famousScores.desc: 克莱门蒂早期最富激情的小调奏鸣曲，预示晚期浪漫主义风格
famousScores.descEn: The most passionate of Clementi's early minor-key sonatas, prefiguring the Romantic era
famousScores.detail:
《d小调钢琴奏鸣曲》Op.13 No.4约出版于1784年，与Op.13 No.6 f小调奏鸣曲同属一套，是克莱门蒂早期成熟期在小调奏鸣曲领域的重要探索。d小调是克莱门蒂偏爱的戏剧性调性之一，莫扎特也曾在这一调性中写下了他的d小调钢琴协奏曲（K.466）和《唐·乔瓦尼》序曲。克莱门蒂的d小调奏鸣曲风格更为直接，以强烈的力度对比和戏剧性的旋律姿态呈现情感冲突。

第一乐章以一个坚定的d小调主题开篇，下行旋律线与强有力的和弦交替出现，建立起一种命运感。在当时的维也纳和伦敦钢琴音乐圈中，这种直接的戏剧性表达方式颇为新颖——与莫扎特更为内敛、精炼的小调风格形成了鲜明对比。第二乐章（如歌的行板）以F大调的明亮色彩带来情绪的舒缓，优美的旋律展示了克莱门蒂在抒情写作上的才华。

将这首奏鸣曲与莫扎特的d小调钢琴协奏曲K.466（同年前后创作）并置对比，是理解18世纪80年代古典主义音乐风格多样性的绝佳视角。两部作品都是d小调，都体现了那个时代最富创造力的两位键盘作曲家面对相同调性时截然不同的审美选择。克莱门蒂的版本以更直接的力量感取胜，而莫扎特则以更深沉的内心独白见长——这一比较恰好说明了为何贝多芬同时从两人身上汲取了营养。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Piano Sonata in D minor Op. 13 No. 4 was published around 1784, part of the same Op. 13 set as the F minor No. 6, and represents an equally important early exploration of the minor-key sonata. D minor was one of Clementi's favored dramatic keys — a preference he shared with Mozart, who used it for his Piano Concerto K. 466 and the Don Giovanni overture. Clementi's D minor style is more direct: strong dynamic contrasts and dramatically gestural melodies present emotional conflict with bracing immediacy.

The first movement opens with a determined D minor theme, alternating a descending melodic line with powerful chords that establish a sense of inexorable fate. This directness of dramatic expression was novel in the keyboard circles of Vienna and London at the time, contrasting markedly with Mozart's more inward, refined minor-key manner. The second movement (Andante cantabile) lightens the mood in F major with an expressive, singing melody that shows Clementi's lyrical gifts at their most natural.

Placing this sonata alongside Mozart's D minor Piano Concerto K. 466 (composed in approximately the same year) offers an ideal lens for understanding the stylistic diversity of Classical music in the 1780s. Both works are in D minor; both reflect the contrasting aesthetic choices of the two greatest keyboard composers of the age when confronted with the same key. Clementi's prevails by direct power; Mozart's by the depth of its interior monologue — a contrast that explains precisely why Beethoven drew nourishment from both simultaneously.

influence: 贝多芬对克莱门蒂的钢琴奏鸣曲推崇备至，认为其优于莫扎特的钢琴作品。克莱门蒂的学生包括约翰·菲尔德（夜曲的发明者，直接影响了肖邦）、卡尔·车尔尼（贝多芬的学生、李斯特的老师）等人，通过这些学生，他的钢琴演奏理念传递到了整个浪漫主义时代。他的钢琴制造事业也推动了钢琴乐器的技术革新。
influenceEn: Beethoven held Clementi's piano sonatas in the highest regard, considering them superior to Mozart's keyboard works. Clementi's pupils included John Field (inventor of the nocturne, who directly influenced Chopin) and Carl Czerny (Beethoven's student and Liszt's teacher), through whom his pianistic ideals were transmitted to the entire Romantic era. His piano manufacturing enterprise also drove important technological innovations in the instrument itself.
